results.reason: GET /health returned HTTP 200 with JSON body {"status":"ok"}; measured latency 0.004882s (<500ms).
GET /schema returned HTTP 200; response contained both action_space and observation_space, and both schemas passed jsonschema meta-schema validation.
POST /reset returned HTTP 200 with required SREObservation keys (metrics, log_buffer, health_status, active_alerts, done, reward); done=false.
POST /step after reset returned HTTP 200 with observation, reward, info, terminated, truncated; contract requires a done field, but response did not include done.
POST /step without prior reset returned HTTP 409 with error detail 'Environment has not been reset. Call POST /reset first.'
WebSocket /ws lifecycle succeeded: reset -> observation, step -> step_result, state -> state with step_count=1, close -> clean disconnect.
Malformed JSON and unknown type both returned error messages while connection remained usable for subsequent valid reset/step messages.
After reset + two step calls, GET /state returned HTTP 200 with required fields and step_count=2 matching executed steps.
Two concurrent WebSocket sessions remained independent: after stepping ws1 only, state1.step_count=1 while state2.step_count=0; subsequent step results differed by session.

commandsRun.observation: Server started on http://127.0.0.1:8000 (startup log confirmed).

commandsRun.observation: http_code=200, has_action_space=True, has_observation_space=True, both schemas meta-schema valid.

commandsRun.observation: http_code=200, missing_keys=[], done_is_false=True, api/order/db keys present.

curl -X POST http://127.0.0.1:8000/step without prior reset

curl reset + two step calls + curl http://127.0.0.1:8000/state in same cookie session + python state check
python concurrent websockets script with two ws connections to ws://127.0.0.1:8000/ws
